Will that continue? Check up the latest trends and developments, daily asset and FX performance, what changed fundamentally and what's coming up in the session ahead.

articles.expand: XAU/USD, XAG/USD Outlook: Gold and Silver bounce higher following global central bank decisions and a sliding US Dollar. articles.title: Asian FX: Oil shock and Fed stance pressure – OCBC

articles.expand: The GBPUSD moved lower in the early European session, testing a key swing area between 1.3446 and 1.3465. That move also dipped below the key 100-day moving average at 1.3465, but sellers couldn’t sustain momentum. The low reached 1.3455 before buyers stepped in and pushed the price higher. articles.title: EUR/GBP slips as ECB and BoE hold rates, but hawks stay alert
articles.expand: The EUR/GBP pair falls some 0.16% on Thursday as the central bank bonanza ends with the European Central Bank (ECB) and the Bank of England (BoE) keeping interest rates unchanged as expected. The cross-pair trades at around 0.8644 after reaching a daily high of 0.8670.

articles.title: ECB sees two rate hikes if Brent stays above $100 – Reuters
articles.expand: Sources speaking with Reuters revealed that the European Central Bank (ECB) policymakers are likely to hike rates “at least” twice this year, beginning in June if there’s no resolution to the Iran conflict.

articles.title: ECB: Stagflation risks complicate policy path – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Global Head of Macro, Carsten Brzeski, notes that the European Central Bank (ECB) kept interest rates on hold as stagflationary pressures in the Eurozone rise. The bank highlights weaker Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth, mixed inflation dynamics and tighter credit conditions.

articles.title: Fed: Dots and easing path steady – ABN AMRO
articles.expand: ABN AMRO’s US Senior Economist Rogier Quaedvlieg reviews the latest Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) decision, noting that the Federal Reserve (Fed) kept rates unchanged and maintained an easing bias in its statement.

articles.title: ECB Recap: Between rising inflation risks and a slowing economy
articles.expand: As widely telegraphed, the European Central Bank (ECB) kept its policy rates unchanged on Thursday, but the tone of the meeting reflected a far more complicated backdrop.

articles.title: Canada: Growth masks structural headwinds – NBC
articles.expand: National Bank of Canada (NBC) economists Matthieu Arseneau and Alexandra Ducharme note that the Canadian economy expanded by 0.2% in February, with Q1 Gross Domestic Product (GDP) by industry tracking 1.7% annualized.

articles.title: GBP/USD surges amid lower-than-expected US GDP growth, BoE hold
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair surges 0.6% on Thursday, trading at around 1.3550 level, after the Bank of England (BoE) left interest rates unchanged at 3.75% with an 8-1 vote and amid lower-than-expected GDP growth in the US.

articles.title: FX picks up again! US Dollar tumbles after the ECB and BOE – EUR/USD, GBP/USD Overview
articles.expand: EUR/USD, GBP/USD update after the Bank of England and ECB: The US Dollar tumbles as FX markets digest a slew of central bank decisions. articles.expand: The Korean won weakened to the 1,480s per dollar as Middle East tensions drove oil prices higher and the Fed's "hawkish hold" reduced rate cut expectations for 2025.
